children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary or the transferred works operating entity may carry out, in accordance with subsection (b) and consistent with existing transfer contracts, any extraordinary operation and maintenance work on a project facility that the Secretary determines to be reasonably required to preserve the structural safety of the project facility.

children.heading: Reimbursement of costs arising from extraordinary operation and maintenance work
children.label: b Reimbursement of costs arising from extraordinary operation and maintenance work

children.heading: Treatment of costs
children.label: 1 Treatment of costs
children.text: For reserved works, costs incurred by the Secretary in conducting extraordinary operation and maintenance work will be allocated to the authorized reimbursable purposes of the project and shall be repaid within 50 years, with interest, from the year in which work undertaken pursuant to this subchapter is substantially complete.

children.heading: Authority of Secretary
children.label: 2 Authority of Secretary
children.text: For transferred works, the Secretary is authorized to advance the costs incurred by the transferred works operating entity in conducting extraordinary operation and maintenance work and negotiate appropriate 50-year repayment contracts with project beneficiaries providing for the return of reimbursable costs, with interest, under this subsection: Provided, however, That no contract entered into pursuant to this subchapter shall be deemed to be a new or amended contract for the purposes of .section 390cc(a) of this title

children.heading: Determination of interest rate
children.label: 3 Determination of interest rate
children.text: The interest rate used for computing interest on work in progress and interest on the unpaid balance of the reimbursable costs of extraordinary operation and maintenance work authorized by this subchapter shall be determined by the Secretary of the Treasury, as of the beginning of the fiscal year in which extraordinary operation and maintenance work is commenced, on the basis of average market yields on outstanding marketable obligations of the United States with the remaining periods of maturity comparable to the applicable reimbursement period of the project, adjusted to the nearest ⅛ of 1 percent on the unamortized balance of any portion of the loan.

children.heading: Emergency extraordinary operation and maintenance work
children.label: c Emergency extraordinary operation and maintenance work

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary or the transferred works operating entity shall carry out any emergency extraordinary operation and maintenance work on a project facility that the Secretary determines to be necessary to minimize the risk of imminent harm to public health or safety, or property.

children.heading: Reimbursement
children.label: 2 Reimbursement
children.text: The Secretary may advance funds for emergency extraordinary operation and maintenance work and shall seek reimbursement from the transferred works operating entity or benefitting entity upon receiving a written assurance from the governing body of such entity that it will negotiate a contract pursuant to this section for repayment of costs incurred by the Secretary in undertaking such work.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 3 Funding
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a project facility inspected and maintained pursuant to the guidelines and criteria set forth in requires extraordinary operation and maintenance pursuant to paragraph (1), the Secretary may provide Federal funds on a nonreimbursable basis sufficient to cover 35 percent of the cost of the extraordinary operation and maintenance allocable to the transferred works operating entity, which is needed to minimize the risk of imminent harm. The remaining share of the Federal funds advanced by the Secretary for such work shall be repaid under subsection (b).section 510a(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: any amounts that are specifically appropriated to the Account under ; andsection 510d of this title

children.label: B
children.text: any amounts deposited in the Account under paragraph (3)(B).

children.heading: Expenditures
children.label: 2 Expenditures
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (3) and (6), the Secretary may expend amounts in the Account to fund and provide for extended repayment of the funds for eligible projects identified in a report submitted under paragraph (5)(B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may not expend amounts under paragraph (2) with respect to an eligible project described in that paragraph unless the transferred works operating entity or project beneficiary responsible for repayment of reimbursable costs has entered into a contract to repay the amounts under subsection (b)(2).

children.heading: Deposit of repaid funds
children.label: B Deposit of repaid funds
children.text: Amounts repaid by a transferred works operating entity or project beneficiary responsible for repayment of reimbursable costs receiving funds under a repayment contract entered into under this subsection shall be deposited in the Account and shall be available to the Secretary for expenditure, subject to paragraph (6), in accordance with this subsection, and without further appropriation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Beginning with fiscal year 2022, not less than once per fiscal year, the Secretary shall accept, during an application period established by the Secretary, applications from transferred works operating entities or project beneficiaries responsible for payment of reimbursable costs for funds and extended repayment for eligible projects.

children.label: i

children.label: ii
children.text: is for the major, non-recurring maintenance of a mission-critical asset; and

children.label: iii
children.text: is not eligible to be carried out or funded under the repayment provisions of .section 508(c) of this title

children.label: ii

children.label: iii

children.label: iv

children.label: v
children.text: the financial justification for requesting an extended repayment period; and

children.label: vi

children.text: describes the results of the Secretary’s review of each application under paragraph (4)(D), including a determination of whether the project is eligible;

children.text: the eligible project;

children.label: II

children.label: III
children.text: any remaining engineering or environmental compliance that is required before the eligible project commences;

children.label: IV

children.label: V

children.label: I

children.label: II

children.label: iii

children.label: D
children.text: describes the allocation of funds from deposits into the Account under paragraph (3)(B); and

children.label: E
children.text: describes the balance of funds in the Account as of the date of the report.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Appropriations Acts may provide for alternate allocation of amounts reported pursuant to paragraph (5)(D) that are made available under this subsection.

children.heading: No alternate allocations
children.label: i No alternate allocations
children.text: If Congress has not enacted legislation establishing alternate allocations by the date on which the Act making full-year appropriations for energy and water development and related agencies for the applicable fiscal year is enacted into law, amounts made available under paragraph (1) shall be allocated by the Secretary.

children.heading: Insufficient alternate allocations
children.label: ii Insufficient alternate allocations
children.text: If Congress enacts legislation establishing alternate allocations for amounts made available under paragraph (1) that are less than the full amount appropriated under that paragraph, the difference between the amount appropriated and the alternate allocation shall be allocated by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: any funding provided, or contracts entered into, under subsection (a) before ; orDecember 27, 2020

children.label: B
children.text: the use of funds otherwise made available to the Secretary to carry out subsection (a).
